Category: Education & Public Sector: Education, Government Services, Non-Profit organizations
Who is this for: Program managers, aid workers, humanitarian coordinators, nonprofit administrators, government service coordinators, public sector managers, community outreach specialists, social services coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Humanitarian organizations, Non-profit organizations, Government Services, Public Administration, Social Services, Community Development, International Development
Description: When flawed data leads to unequal or delayed support.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how aid organisations operate and a general interest in how technology is used in public services are all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify common sources of flawed data in humanitarian aid systems and explain how these inaccuracies can lead to unequal or delayed support for vulnerable communities. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise and address data quality issues, helping to ensure that AI tools are used responsibly and fairly within aid distribution programmes.
